critic_raw:
Issue 1
Claim: The AutoML system outperforms the logistic baseline on ROC-AUC and lift at the operating threshold.
Why wrong: Target encoding and median imputation were applied to the full two-year modeling table before cross-validation folds were created. Target encoding computes the mean of the target variable within each category and assigns it as a feature. When computed on the full dataset, the target encoding for a given category in the training fold implicitly encodes outcome information from the validation fold cases in that category. This is a classic label-leakage path through high-cardinality categorical features. The ROC-AUC from this cross-validation is not an unbiased estimate of hold-out performance.
Evidence: Rebuild the preprocessing pipeline so that target encoding is computed only on the training fold within each cross-validation split. Re-evaluate the AutoML search under this leakage-free design and compare ROC-AUC.

Issue 2
Claim: The final operating threshold was chosen to achieve 80% recall on the held-out test set.
Why wrong: Choosing the threshold on the test set turns the test set into a threshold selection tool — it is no longer an independent evaluation of model performance at a specific operating point. The reported 80% recall and the corresponding precision on the held-out test set are optimistic estimates because the threshold was selected to maximize a criterion on that same data. If this precision estimate is used to justify deploying the model at this threshold, it is likely an overestimate of production performance.
Evidence: Hold out a separate calibration set for threshold selection and report precision and recall on a truly independent test set after threshold is fixed.

Issue 3
Claim: The comparison between AutoML and the logistic baseline is fair.
Why wrong: The AutoML challenger benefits from full-data target encoding and test-set threshold selection. The logistic baseline uses its original threshold selected from a prior validation set under a different protocol. The comparison is asymmetric in both preprocessing methodology and threshold selection procedure. The baseline would likely show different performance if re-evaluated under the same preprocessing pipeline and threshold selection protocol as the challenger.
Evidence: Re-run the logistic regression baseline inside the same pipeline (fold-specific target encoding, threshold selected from validation data for both systems) and compare on the independent test set.
defender_raw: The AutoML system includes meaningful feature engineering improvements through target encoding for high-cardinality fields. The ROC-AUC improvement is sizable and the lift at the operating threshold is clear. Target encoding is a legitimate technique that captures categorical predictive information. The baseline logistic regression uses simpler features (one-hot encoding) that limit its capacity to capture category-level information. The 80% recall threshold is an operational requirement from the investigations team — it is not a free parameter being optimized. Using the test set to identify the threshold that achieves this required recall level is a practical necessity.
adjudication_raw: The defender's response on all three issues is weak. On Issue 1: the defender defends target encoding as a legitimate technique, which it is — the critique is not that target encoding is wrong but that it was applied before fold creation. These are distinct claims and the defender conflates them. On Issue 2: the defender says 80% recall is an 'operational requirement' — but even if the target recall is fixed operationally, the threshold that achieves it can be selected on the validation set rather than the test set. Using the test set for threshold selection contaminates the precision estimate regardless of whether the recall target was predetermined. On Issue 3: the asymmetric protocol is not addressed at all. All three issues stand. Verdict: critique_wins.
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: target encoding computed on full dataset before fold creation leaks label information into evaluation
Issue 2: operating threshold selected on test set contaminates precision estimate
Issue 3: asymmetric preprocessing and threshold protocol between AutoML and baseline

empirical_test.condition: Rebuild AutoML pipeline with fold-specific target encoding; select threshold on validation set; re-run baseline under same protocol; compare both on an independent held-out test set
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: AutoML lift at 80% recall shrinks below 1 percentage point or reverses when leakage-free protocol applied
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: AutoML still improves ROC-AUC by at least 0.02 and precision at 80% recall by at least 3 percentage points under leakage-free protocol
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: ROC-AUC gain preserved but queue-level threshold gain disappears, or vice versa
